name: Deviled Crab Quiche
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C).
2. In a bowl, combine flour, salt, and chili seasoning.
3. Cut in margarine and lard until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs.
4. Add cheese and mix well. Stir in cold water and mix to form a fairly firm dough.
5. Knead gently on a floured surface and roll out pastry.
6. Set a 10-inch fluted tart pan with a removable bottom on a baking sheet.
7. Press pastry into the molds and trim the edges neatly.
8. Prick the base with a fork.
9. Line the pastry with waxed paper and fill with dried beans.
10. Bake for 15 minutes. Remove the waxed paper and beans, then bake an additional 5-10 minutes or until dry and lightly golden.
11. Fry bacon for 3 minutes. Add onions and cook for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and mix with crabmeat.
12. Spoon the mixture into the flan shell.
13. Whisk eggs, half-and-half, mustard, cayenne, and salt.
14. Pour the mixture into the flan shell.
15. Bake for 30-35 minutes or until set and lightly golden.
16. Garnish with tomato slices and parsley if desired, and serve warm or cold.
